etymology.description: 攀(攀) (pān, "climb") + 岩(岩) (yán, "rock") combine to convey the concept of climbing rocks, which directly describes the activity of rock climbing.
etymology.interpretation: 攀(攀) (pān, "climb") denotes the action of climbing or ascending, often requiring effort and agility. 岩(岩) (yán, "rock") specifically refers to large rocks or cliffs. Together, they vividly illustrate the activity of scaling rock faces, which captures the essence of rock climbing.

examples.chinese: 攀岩(攀岩)是一项很具挑战性的运动(是一項很具挑戰性的運動)。
examples.pinyin: Pānyán shì yī xiàng hěn jù tiǎozhàn xìng de yùndòng.
examples.english: Rock climbing is a very challenging sport.

examples.chinese: 昨天(昨天)，我和朋友去攀岩(我和朋友去攀岩)。
examples.pinyin: Zuótiān, wǒ hé péngyǒu qù pānyán.
examples.english: Yesterday, my friend and I went rock climbing.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢在假期中(他喜歡在假期中)，到不同的地方去攀岩(到不同的地方去攀岩)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài jiàqī zhōng, dào bùtóng de dìfāng qù pānyán.
examples.english: He likes to go rock climbing in different places during vacations.
